req.title: Open savings account
req.statement: A verified customer shall open a savings account with a minimum balance of $100 and tiered interest rates applied automatically.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-002 mandates that verified customers can open savings accounts with $100+ minimum balance, automatic tier assignment, and 422 rejection below threshold. The test suite covers: (1) happy path with valid balance and tier assignment, (2) negative path with insufficient balance rejection, (3) boundary cases at minimum threshold ($100 and $99.99). Heuristics applied: EP partitions verified/unverified and balance sufficiency; BVA tests $100 and $99.99 boundaries; NEG validates error handling; STC confirms account creation state transition.

tests.name: test_open_savings_account_valid_balance_tier_assigned
trace: R-FIN-002: A verified customer shall open a savings account with minimum balance of $100
R-FIN-002: tiered interest rates applied automatically
R-FIN-002: Expected outcomes: account created; tier assigned
tests.executable:
def test_open_savings_account_valid_balance_tier_assigned():
    import requests
    api_base = "http://banking-api"
    customer_id = "cust_verified_001"
    payload = {"customer_id": customer_id, "initial_balance": 250.00}
    resp = requests.post(f"{api_base}/accounts/savings", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    data = resp.json()
    assert "account_id" in data
    assert data.get("interest_tier") is not None
    assert data.get("status") == "active"
    assert data.get("balance") == 250.00

tests.name: test_open_savings_account_insufficient_balance_422
actions: Capture response status and error message
expected: account is not created in system
trace: R-FIN-002: Negative test validates constraint enforcement
tests.executable:
def test_open_savings_account_insufficient_balance_422():
    import requests
    api_base = "http://banking-api"
    customer_id = "cust_verified_002"
    payload = {"customer_id": customer_id, "initial_balance": 50.00}
    resp = requests.post(f"{api_base}/accounts/savings", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    data = resp.json()
    assert "error_code" in data
    assert "minimum_balance" in data.get("error_code", "").lower()
    assert "account_id" not in data

tests.name: test_open_savings_account_boundary_minimum_100_dollars
preconditions: Initial balance exactly at minimum threshold: $100.00
actions: Capture response status and account details
trace: R-FIN-002: Boundary Value Analysis at minimum threshold
R-FIN-002: minimum balance of $100 shall allow account creation
ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4: BVA test on inclusive lower bound
tests.executable:
def test_open_savings_account_boundary_minimum_100_dollars():
    import requests
    api_base = "http://banking-api"
    customer_id = "cust_verified_003"
    payload = {"customer_id": customer_id, "initial_balance": 100.00}
    resp = requests.post(f"{api_base}/accounts/savings", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    data = resp.json()
    assert data.get("balance") == 100.00
    assert "account_id" in data
    assert data.get("interest_tier") is not None
    assert data.get("status") == "active"

tests.name: test_open_savings_account_boundary_just_below_minimum
preconditions: Initial balance just below minimum threshold: $99.99
actions: Capture response status and error details
expected: Error indicates minimum balance requirement not met
trace: R-FIN-002: Boundary Value Analysis just below minimum threshold
ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4: BVA test on exclusive lower bound
tests.executable:
def test_open_savings_account_boundary_just_below_minimum():
    import requests
    api_base = "http://banking-api"
    customer_id = "cust_verified_004"
    payload = {"customer_id": customer_id, "initial_balance": 99.99}
    resp = requests.post(f"{api_base}/accounts/savings", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    data = resp.json()
    assert "account_id" not in data
    assert "error" in data or "error_code" in data

tests.name: test_open_savings_account_unverified_customer_rejection
preconditions: Banking API validates customer verification status
actions: POST /accounts/savings with unverified customer and initial_balance=500.00
Capture response status and error message
expected: HTTP status 403 Forbidden or 400 Bad Request
Error message references customer verification requirement
trace: R-FIN-002: Decision Table test: verified=false => reject
R-FIN-002: Negative test validates precondition enforcement
tests.executable:
def test_open_savings_account_unverified_customer_rejection():
    import requests
    api_base = "http://banking-api"
    customer_id = "cust_unverified_001"
    payload = {"customer_id": customer_id, "initial_balance": 500.00, "verified": False}
    resp = requests.post(f"{api_base}/accounts/savings", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code in [403, 400]
    data = resp.json()
    assert "account_id" not in data
